claims.claimText: Congress had a bipartisan plan to let troops fix their own equipment.

claims.claimText: More than 70 percent of corporate laptops that enter e-waste pipelines still have four to six years of usable life left
claims.postExcerpt: Right to Repair is trending everywhere. But there is one truth the entire industry avoids. Most laptops do not die. They are killed by poor repair, cheap components, and zero accountability. And once a laptop struggles, the reflex action is to buy a new one. This habit drains India’s wallets by thou

claims.claimText: Right-to-repair language for military equipment was stripped from the NDAA in December 2025
claims.postExcerpt: 🇺🇸 Right to repair language for military equipment was stripped from the NDAA in December despite White House, Pentagon, and bipartisan backing. Atlantic Council analysis says the current repair restrictions threaten U.S. forces combat readiness.

claims.claimText: The White House and the Pentagon backed right-to-repair language for military equipment in the NDAA

claims.claimText: Atlantic Council analysis says current repair restrictions threaten U.S. forces' combat readiness
claims.postExcerpt: 🇺🇸 Right to repair language for military equipment was stripped from the NDAA in December despite White House, Pentagon, and bipartisan backing. Atlantic Council analysis says the current repair restrictions threaten U.S. forces combat readiness.

claims.claimText: The right-to-repair movement received a boost in 2025 from big tech, tariffs, and an economic downturn
claims.postExcerpt: This year, the right-to-repair movement got a boost from—surprisingly—big tech, tariffs, and economic downturn. But the companies controlling who fixes their stuff aren’t giving up that power willingly.

claims.claimText: Companies that control who fixes their products are not willingly giving up that repair power
claims.postExcerpt: This year, the right-to-repair movement got a boost from—surprisingly—big tech, tariffs, and economic downturn. But the companies controlling who fixes their stuff aren’t giving up that power willingly.

claims.claimText: The U.S. House has a bill called the Repair Act
claims.postExcerpt: As vehicles grow more software-dependent, repairing them has become harder than ever. A bill in the US House called the Repair Act would ease those restrictions, but it comes with caveats.
